title: Runtime integration: effort override, deferred fields, and slash commands
purpose: Skills can override the agent's reasoning effort for the activation turn, store deferred fields (allowed-tools, fork mode) as metadata, and register as slash commands for user invocation. This brief combines the runtime integration features: SkillEffort-to-ReasoningEffort mapping, effort override lifecycle on LoopContext, allowed-tools as a stored hint, fork mode documentation, slash command registration from the catalog, and the invocation control matrix.
task: Add SkillEffort-to-ReasoningEffort mapping (max maps to Max). Add override/restore methods on LoopContext for effort. Store allowed-tools as hint with diagnostic. Document fork mode semantics (skill body = task input). Register user_invocable skills as slash commands via SlashCommandRegistry. Implement the invocation control matrix: user_invocable controls slash command visibility, disable_model_invocation controls catalog listing visibility.

requirements.title: SkillEffort to ReasoningEffort mapping
requirements.spec: skill/types.rs SHALL provide a method or From impl that maps SkillEffort to ReasoningEffort: Low->Low, Medium->Medium, High->High, XHigh->XHigh, Max->Max.
acceptance: SkillEffort::Low maps to ReasoningEffort::Low
SkillEffort::Medium maps to ReasoningEffort::Medium
SkillEffort::High maps to ReasoningEffort::High
SkillEffort::XHigh maps to ReasoningEffort::XHigh
SkillEffort::Max maps to ReasoningEffort::Max

requirements.title: Effort override on LoopContext
requirements.spec: WHEN a skill with an effort field is invoked, LoopContext reasoning_effort SHALL be overridden with the mapped value. The previous value SHALL be captured. After the activation turn's provider call completes, the previous value SHALL be restored.
acceptance: After applying effort override, LoopContext.reasoning_effort is Some(mapped_value)
The previous reasoning_effort value is returned for restoration
Restoring returns LoopContext.reasoning_effort to its original state
A skill without effort field does not modify LoopContext.reasoning_effort

requirements.title: allowed-tools stored as hint
requirements.spec: The allowed_tools field SHALL be stored in SkillMetadata (done in NS-001) but NOT enforced at runtime. A diagnostic SHALL be recorded noting that allowed-tools is stored but not enforced.
acceptance: A skill with allowed-tools: 'Bash(git *) Read' has the field stored in metadata
No tool filtering or permission changes occur when the skill is activated
A diagnostic is recorded stating allowed-tools is not enforced in current version

requirements.title: Fork mode documentation and metadata
requirements.spec: Fork mode metadata (context: fork, agent field) SHALL be stored in SkillMetadata. When fork execution is wired (future), the agent field SHALL select the subagent configuration (system prompt, tools); the expanded skill body SHALL be the task input, NOT the system prompt.
acceptance: A skill with context: fork and agent: Explore has both fields stored correctly
No fork execution occurs in this brief (deferred to agent wiring)
The types.rs documentation clearly states skill body = task, not system prompt

requirements.title: Slash command registration from catalog
requirements.spec: WHEN building the SlashCommandRegistry, each skill in the SkillCatalog where user_invocable is true SHALL be registered as a slash command named after the skill.
acceptance: A skill 'deploy' with user_invocable: true is registered as /deploy
A skill 'background-knowledge' with user_invocable: false is NOT registered
Slash command names match skill names exactly

requirements.title: Slash command handler invokes SkillTool
requirements.spec: WHEN a skill slash command is invoked with text (e.g. /deploy prod), the handler SHALL call the SkillTool with name set to the skill name and arguments set to the trailing text.
acceptance: /deploy prod calls SkillTool with name='deploy' and arguments='prod'
/deploy (no args) calls SkillTool with name='deploy' and arguments absent
The handler returns the SkillTool's output to the model

requirements.title: Invocation control matrix
requirements.spec: The invocation control SHALL follow three modes: (1) default — slash command AND in catalog listing. (2) disable_model_invocation: true — slash command, NOT in listing. (3) user_invocable: false — no slash command, in listing.
acceptance: Default skill: slash command registered AND in system_prompt_listing()
disable_model_invocation: true: slash command registered, NOT in listing
user_invocable: false: no slash command, appears in listing

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement fork/subagent execution — deferred to norn-agents wiring
SHALL NOT implement per-skill hooks — deferred to Phase 2
SHALL NOT implement path-scoped activation — deferred to Phase 2
SHALL NOT enforce allowed-tools as restriction or pre-approval
SHALL NOT modify the SlashCommandRegistry internals — use its public API
SHALL NOT wire into build_runtime — that is NS-006
verification: cargo test -p norn -- skill passes all tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
